message.content:
You are an adversarial observability and provenance auditor. Audit /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay for OBSERVABILITY and PROVENANCE issues.

Focus areas:
- OpenTelemetry: are spans started for every pipeline phase? Are they closed on error?
- MLflow: is every run ended even on exception? Are nested runs handled correctly?
- Phoenix: is tracing initialised before the first span is created?
- Provenance trail: is every pipeline action recorded with enough context?
- Hash chain: is the chain integrity verifiable end-to-end? Is there a verify() path?
- Transcript capture: is the pre-commit hook robust? Can it fail silently?
- Structured logging: are errors logged with enough context?
- Metrics: are there any SLO-relevant metrics?

Read src/assay_engine/observability/, src/assay_engine/provenance.py, scripts/, .githooks/ carefully.

Return findings as JSON with id (OB-N), severity, file, line, title, description, poc, suggested_fix.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness â€” fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly â€” if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; highâ†’max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only â€” it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK â€” model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER â€” read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" â€” whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) â€” never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named â€” don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Self-hosted tracing â€” OpenTelemetry â†’ a local collector (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's Phoenix/OTel bootstrap. Everything is
4	on-box: spans export over OTLP to a loopback-bound collector; nothing leaves the machine.
5	OpenTelemetry and the Phoenix collector are optional (the ``observability`` extra) and
6	imported lazily, so this module loads â€” and the engine runs untraced â€” with neither present.
7	
8	The reasoning seam already emits spans through the global OTel tracer provider; calling
9	:func:`bootstrap_tracing` once at startup wires that global provider at a local collector so
10	those spans land somewhere. Without the extra (or with ``ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING`` set),
11	tracing degrades to a silent no-op.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import atexit
17	import logging
18	import os
19	import signal
20	import threading
21	from contextlib import contextmanager
22	from typing import Any, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol
23	
24	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
25	
26	_log = logging.getLogger("assay_engine.observability")
27	
28	# Local collector endpoint (loopback-enforced).
29	TRACING_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_HOST", "localhost")
30	TRACING_PORT = int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PORT", "6006"))
31	PROJECT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PROJECT", "assay")
32	
33	_provider: Any = None
34	# Guards the _provider check-then-set so concurrent bootstrap_tracing() calls don't race.
35	_provider_lock = threading.Lock()
36	
37	
38	def _disabled() -> bool:
39	    return os.environ.get("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
40	
41	
42	def tracing_base_url() -> str:
43	    require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
44	    return f"http://{TRACING_HOST}:{TRACING_PORT}"
45	
46	
47	def tracing_endpoint() -> str:
48	    """OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint at the local collector (loopback-enforced)."""
49	    return f"{tracing_base_url()}/v1/traces"
50	
51	
52	def bootstrap_tracing() -> Any:
53	    """Idempotently wire the global OTel tracer provider to the local collector.
54	
55	    Returns the provider, or ``None`` if tracing is disabled, the extra is not installed, or
56	    setup fails â€” tracing must never be load-bearing, so this degrades gracefully.
57	    """
58	    global _provider
59	    if _disabled():
60	        return None
61	    # Lock the check-then-set so two threads can't both register the global provider.
62	    with _provider_lock:
63	        if _provider is not None:
64	            return _provider
65	        try:
66	            require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
67	            # Bound the OTLP export so a downed collector can never hang teardown (#O3).
68	            os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
69	            from phoenix.otel import register
70	
71	            provider = register(
72	                project_name=PROJECT_NAME,
73	                endpoint=tracing_endpoint(),
74	                protocol="http/protobuf",  # pin: endpoint is .../v1/traces â€” never flip to gRPC
75	                set_global_tracer_provider=True,
76	                batch=True,  # async BatchSpanProcessor â€” never block the run on export (#O2)
77	                verbose=False,  # no stdout banner from a library context
78	            )
79	            _provider = provider
80	            _warn_if_not_global(provider)
81	            _instrument_langchain()
82	            _install_flush_on_exit(provider)
83	            return _provider
84	        except Exception:
85	            # Missing extra / collector down / registration race â€” run untraced.
86	            return None
87	
88	
89	def _warn_if_not_global(provider: Any) -> None:
90	    """If another provider already won the global slot, our manual spans would vanish (#O6)."""
91	    try:
92	        from opentelemetry import trace
93	
94	        if trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
95	            _log.warning(
96	                "assay tracing: another OpenTelemetry provider holds the global slot; "
97	                "manually-emitted spans may not reach the local collector"
98	            )
99	    except Exception:
100	        pass
101	
102	
103	def _instrument_langchain() -> None:
104	    """Capture LangChain auto-spans (the bulk reasoning tier uses LangChain) â€” best-effort (#O5)."""
105	    try:
106	        from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
107	
108	        LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(skip_dep_check=True)
109	    except Exception:
110	        pass
111	
112	
113	def _install_flush_on_exit(provider: Any) -> None:
114	    """Flush buffered spans on clean exit (atexit) and on SIGTERM (#O3).
115	
116	    atexit does not run on ``kill``/container-stop, so a main-thread SIGTERM handler also
117	    flushes+shuts down. Both are guarded â€” tracing teardown must never raise.
118	    """
119	    flush = getattr(provider, "force_flush", None)
120	    if not callable(flush):
121	        return
122	    atexit.register(lambda: _safe(flush))
123	    if threading.current_thread() is threading.main_thread():
124	        try:
125	            prior = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)
126	
127	            def _handler(signum: int, frame: Any) -> None:
128	                _safe(flush)
129	                _safe(getattr(provider, "shutdown", lambda: None))
130	                if callable(prior):
131	                    prior(signum, frame)
132	
133	            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
134	        except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - not on main thread / unsupported
135	            pass
136	
137	
138	def _safe(fn: Any) -> None:
139	    try:
140	        fn()
141	    except Exception:
142	        pass
143	
144	
145	@contextmanager
146	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None) -> Iterator[None]:
147	    """Stamp ``run_id`` onto every span emitted inside the block (cross-store correlation).
148	
149	    A no-op when ``run_id`` is None or OpenTelemetry is absent.
150	    """
151	    if run_id is None:
152	        yield
153	        return
154	    try:
155	        from opentelemetry import baggage, context
156	    except ImportError:
157	        yield
158	        return
159	    token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("assay.run_id", run_id))
160	    try:
161	        yield
162	    finally:
163	        context.detach(token)
164	
165	
166	# OpenInference requires every span carry a span kind so Phoenix classifies it (CHAIN/LLM/
167	# AGENT/TOOL); a plain OTel span with no kind renders as UNKNOWN in the Phoenix UI. We set the
168	# raw semantic-convention attribute directly (no openinference import needed for a manual span).
169	_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND = "openinference.span.kind"
170	
171	
172	# Structural Protocol only â€” adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
173	class Tracer(Protocol):
174	    @contextmanager
175	    def span(
176	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
177	    ) -> Iterator[None]: ...
178	
179	
180	class OtelTracer:
181	    """A tracer that emits OpenTelemetry spans via the global provider (no-op if OTel absent).
182	
183	    Use this where a component wants explicit spans rather than relying on auto-instrumentation;
184	    it honors whatever provider :func:`bootstrap_tracing` registered. Each span is stamped with
185	    its OpenInference ``kind`` so Phoenix classifies it (default ``CHAIN``).
186	    """
187	
188	    @contextmanager
189	    def span(
190	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
191	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
192	        try:
193	            from opentelemetry import trace
194	        except ImportError:
195	            yield
196	            return
197	        tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine")
198	        with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as sp:
199	            sp.set_attribute(_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND, kind)
200	            for k, v in (attributes or {}).items():
201	                sp.set_attribute(k, v)
202	            yield
203	
204	
205	class UnconfiguredTracer:
206	    """Fail-loud placeholder for contexts that must not silently drop spans."""
207	
208	    @contextmanager
209	    def span(
210	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
211	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
212	        raise NotImplementedError(
213	            "no tracer configured â€” call bootstrap_tracing() and use OtelTracer, or supply a "
214	            "test double"
215	        )
216	        yield  # pragma: no cover - keeps this a generator/contextmanager
217

1	"""Experiment tracking â€” a local, on-box run/metric/artifact store (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's MLflow setup. The backend is a local store
4	(default: a SQLite file under the working directory); a remote tracking server is rejected by
5	the local-URI guard. MLflow is optional (the ``observability`` extra) and imported lazily, so
6	this module loads without it.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
16	
17	_DEFAULT_URI = "sqlite:///assay_mlflow.db"
18	EXPERIMENT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_EXPERIMENT", "assay")
19	
20	
21	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
22	    """Resolve the tracking URI (env override, else local SQLite), enforced local."""
23	    uri = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", _DEFAULT_URI)
24	    # Resolve a relative sqlite path to an absolute file so runs land deterministically.
25	    prefix = "sqlite:///"
26	    if uri.startswith(prefix):
27	        rel = uri[len(prefix) :]
28	        if not os.path.isabs(rel):
29	            uri = prefix + str((Path.cwd() / rel).resolve())
30	    return require_local_uri(uri, what="experiment tracking")
31	
32	
33	# Structural Protocol only â€” adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
34	class ExperimentTracker(Protocol):
35	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str: ...
36	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None: ...
37	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None: ...
38	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None: ...
39	
40	
41	class MlflowExperimentTracker:
42	    """A local-store experiment tracker backed by MLflow (lazy import).
43	
44	    Construction validates the tracking URI is local and selects the experiment; it does not
45	    import MLflow until a run is started, so the module is importable without the extra.
46	    """
47	
48	    def __init__(self, experiment: str = EXPERIMENT_NAME) -> None:
49	        self._experiment = experiment
50	        self._uri = get_tracking_uri()  # validates local before anything else
51	
52	    def _client(self) -> Any:
53	        """An MlflowClient bound to the local store. Uses the client API (keyed by run_id)
54	        throughout, so no run ever sits on the global active-run stack â€” start_run followed by
55	        log_metric cannot collide (audit issue #O1)."""
56	        try:
57	            from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
58	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
59	            raise RuntimeError(
60	                "experiment tracking requires the 'observability' extra (mlflow) â€” not installed"
61	            ) from exc
62	        return MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self._uri)
63	
64	    def _experiment_id(self, client: Any) -> str:
65	        exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
66	        if exp is not None:
67	            return str(exp.experiment_id)
68	        return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
69	
70	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
71	        client = self._client()
72	        run = client.create_run(self._experiment_id(client), run_name=name)
73	        run_id = str(run.info.run_id)
74	        for k, v in dict(params).items():
75	            client.log_param(run_id, k, v)
76	        return run_id
77	
78	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None:
79	        self._client().log_metric(run_id, key, value)
80	
81	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None:
82	        self._client().log_artifact(run_id, path)
83	
84	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None:
85	        # MLflow run statuses: FINISHED / FAILED / KILLED. Recording FAILED makes a failed run
86	        # distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110).
87	        self._client().set_terminated(run_id, status=status)
88

1	"""Append-only provenance trail (GOVERNANCE Â§3, W3C PROV-DM style).
2	
3	Every action a study takes is recorded, in order, before the next executes: ingestion, the
4	blind baseline build, each discovered/locked hypothesis, every gate decision, each verdict,
5	the source scorecard, the final report. The trail is the spine of reproducibility and of the
6	independence claim â€” "no step can be retroactively removed or reordered" (GOVERNANCE Â§3).
7	
8	The trail enforces that structurally, not by promise:
9	
10	- **Append-only.** Entries are added through :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.record` only; there is no
11	  remove/edit/reorder API and the exposed view is an immutable tuple of frozen entries.
12	- **Chained.** Each entry carries ``prev_hash`` and an ``entry_hash`` over
13	  ``(prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp)`` using the engine's one type-faithful
14	  serializer (:mod:`assay_engine._canonical`), rooted at a fixed genesis.
15	
16	Integrity â€” honest scope, two tiers:
17	
18	- **Unkeyed (default).** The chain is plain SHA-256. It is *tamper-evident against naive
19	  tampering and accidental corruption*: editing one entry without recomputing downstream
20	  hashes, reordering, or deleting an entry is caught by :func:`verify_records`. It is **not**
21	  forgery-proof: a motivated party who controls the serialized bytes can edit a payload and
22	  recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain, and the result verifies. Unkeyed integrity buys
23	  "is this a valid chain?", not "is this *the* chain the engine produced."
24	- **Keyed (``secret=...``).** Pass an on-box secret (as pre-registration does, ADR-0009) and
25	  the chain is HMAC-SHA256. The head cannot be recomputed without the secret, so a downstream
26	  store cannot silently rewrite history and re-seal it; :func:`verify_records` must be given
27	  the same secret. This is local tamper-*resistance*; non-repudiable third-party attestation of
28	  the trail's time remains the same pluggable concern as pre-registration, out of scope here.
29	"""
30	
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import datetime as _dt
34	import hashlib
35	import hmac
36	import threading
37	from dataclasses import dataclass
38	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping
39	
40	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_json
41	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
42	
43	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
44	_GENESIS = "assay-provenance:v1:genesis"
45	
46	Clock = Callable[[], _dt.datetime]
47	
48	
49	class ProvenanceError(RuntimeError):
50	    """The provenance trail is inconsistent (a broken chain) or an entry cannot be recorded."""
51	
52	
53	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
54	class ProvenanceEntry:
55	    """One immutable, chained record of a single action."""
56	
57	    seq: int
58	    kind: str
59	    summary: str
60	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
61	    timestamp: str  # ISO-8601 UTC
62	    prev_hash: str
63	    entry_hash: str
64	
65	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
66	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
67	
68	
69	def _digest(
70	    seq: int,
71	    kind: str,
72	    summary: str,
73	    payload: Mapping[str, Any],
74	    timestamp: str,
75	    prev_hash: str,
76	    secret: bytes | None,
77	) -> str:
78	    try:
79	        msg = canonical_json(
80	            ["assay-prov:v1", prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, dict(payload), timestamp]
81	        ).encode("utf-8")
82	    except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
83	        # RecursionError (deeply nested payload) is a RuntimeError, not Value/TypeError â€” catch it
84	        # too so the entrypoints surface a typed ProvenanceError, never a raw exception (#89).
85	        raise ProvenanceError(
86	            f"provenance payload for {kind!r} cannot be serialized reproducibly ({exc}); "
87	            "record only JSON-native / hashable values of bounded depth"
88	        ) from None
89	    if secret is None:
90	        return hashlib.sha256(msg).hexdigest()
91	    return hmac.new(secret, msg, "sha256").hexdigest()
92	
93	
94	def _utc_now() -> _dt.datetime:
95	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
96	
97	
98	class ProvenanceTrail:
99	    """An append-only, chained audit trail for one study run.
100	
101	    ``secret`` (>= 16 bytes) keys the chain with HMAC so a serialized trail cannot be forged
102	    without it (see module docstring). ``clock`` injects the timestamp source (deterministic
103	    tests); it must return a timezone-aware ``datetime``.
104	    """
105	
106	    def __init__(self, *, secret: bytes | None = None, clock: Clock | None = None) -> None:
107	        if secret is not None and (not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16):
108	            raise ValueError("provenance secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
109	        self._entries: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
110	        self._secret: bytes | None = bytes(secret) if secret is not None else None
111	        self._clock: Clock = clock or _utc_now
112	        # serialize the read-compute-append so concurrent record() calls cannot interleave and
113	        # corrupt the seq/prev_hash chain (#114)
114	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
115	
116	    def record(self, kind: str, summary: str, **payload: Any) -> ProvenanceEntry:
117	        """Append one entry. Returns it. The only way to add to the trail. Thread-safe."""
118	        if not kind:
119	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance entry kind must be non-empty")
120	        with self._lock:
121	            return self._record_locked(kind, summary, payload)
122	
123	    def _record_locked(
124	        self, kind: str, summary: str, payload: Mapping[str, Any]
125	    ) -> ProvenanceEntry:
126	        seq = len(self._entries)
127	        prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
128	        try:
129	            instant = self._clock()
130	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 â€” the clock is an injected dependency
131	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance clock raised: {exc}") from None
132	        if not isinstance(instant, _dt.datetime):
133	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a datetime")
134	        try:
135	            # utcoffset()/astimezone()/isoformat() can raise on a hostile datetime subclass or an
136	            # out-of-range value â€” keep the typed-error contract (#96).
137	            if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
138	                raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a timezone-aware datetime")
139	            timestamp = instant.astimezone(_UTC).isoformat()
140	        except ProvenanceError:
141	            raise
142	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
143	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance timestamp could not be normalized: {exc}") from None
144	        entry_hash = _digest(seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp, prev_hash, self._secret)
145	        try:
146	            entry = ProvenanceEntry(
147	                seq=seq,
148	                kind=kind,
149	                summary=summary,
150	                payload=payload,
151	                timestamp=timestamp,
152	                prev_hash=prev_hash,
153	                entry_hash=entry_hash,
154	            )
155	        except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
156	            raise ProvenanceError(
157	                f"provenance entry for {kind!r} could not be frozen ({exc})"
158	            ) from None
159	        self._entries.append(entry)
160	        return entry
161	
162	    def as_recorder(self) -> Callable[[Any], None]:
163	        """A :data:`gates.ProvenanceRecorder` bound to this trail (records GateDecisions).
164	
165	        Reads each attribute exactly once and defensively, so a booby-trapped decision object
166	        (a property that raises) surfaces a typed ``ProvenanceError`` rather than a raw error.
167	        """
168	
169	        def _record(decision: Any) -> None:
170	            try:
171	                gate = getattr(decision, "gate", None)
172	                approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
173	                reason = getattr(decision, "reason", "")
174	                evidence = dict(getattr(decision, "evidence", {}) or {})
175	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 â€” the decision is on the trust boundary
176	                raise ProvenanceError(
177	                    f"could not read gate decision for provenance: {exc}"
178	                ) from None
179	            self.record(
180	                "gate",
181	                f"gate {gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
182	                gate=gate,
183	                approved=approved,
184	                reason=reason,
185	                evidence=evidence,
186	            )
187	
188	        return _record
189	
190	    @property
191	    def entries(self) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
192	        return tuple(self._entries)
193	
194	    @property
195	    def head(self) -> str:
196	        """The current chain head (last entry_hash, or the genesis if empty)."""
197	        return self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
198	
199	    def __len__(self) -> int:
200	        return len(self._entries)
201	
202	    def verify(self) -> None:
203	        """Recompute the chain; raise :class:`ProvenanceError` on any inconsistency."""
204	        verify_records(self._entries, secret=self._secret)
205	
206	    def to_records(self) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
207	        """Serialize to plain dicts (for a persistent store); re-checkable via :func:`verify_records`."""
208	        return tuple(
209	            {
210	                "seq": e.seq,
211	                "kind": e.kind,
212	                "summary": e.summary,
213	                "payload": dict(e.payload),
214	                "timestamp": e.timestamp,
215	                "prev_hash": e.prev_hash,
216	                "entry_hash": e.entry_hash,
217	            }
218	            for e in self._entries
219	        )
220	
221	
222	def verify_records(
223	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
224	    *,
225	    secret: bytes | None = None,
226	) -> None:
227	    """Verify a sequence of entries forms an intact chain (order, linkage, content).
228	
229	    Pass the same ``secret`` the trail was keyed with; an unkeyed trail verifies with
230	    ``secret=None``. A keyed trail will not verify under the wrong/absent secret.
231	    """
232	    prev = _GENESIS
233	    for i, e in enumerate(entries):
234	        if e.seq != i:
235	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} has seq {e.seq} (reordered or removed)")
236	        if e.prev_hash != prev:
237	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} prev_hash breaks the chain")
238	        recomputed = _digest(e.seq, e.kind, e.summary, e.payload, e.timestamp, e.prev_hash, secret)
239	        if not hmac.compare_digest(recomputed, e.entry_hash):
240	            raise ProvenanceError(
241	                f"provenance entry {i} content was altered, or the wrong key was used (hash mismatch)"
242	            )
243	        prev = e.entry_hash
244	
245	
246	def from_records(
247	    records: "list[Mapping[str, Any]] | tuple[Mapping[str, Any], ...]",
248	    *,
249	    secret: bytes | None = None,
250	) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
251	    """Rebuild entries from :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` output and verify the chain."""
252	    entries = tuple(
253	        ProvenanceEntry(
254	            seq=r["seq"],
255	            kind=r["kind"],
256	            summary=r["summary"],
257	            payload=r["payload"],
258	            timestamp=r["timestamp"],
259	            prev_hash=r["prev_hash"],
260	            entry_hash=r["entry_hash"],
261	        )
262	        for r in records
263	    )
264	    verify_records(entries, secret=secret)
265	    return entries
266

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Capture the active Claude session's JSONL transcript(s) into the repo for provenance.
3	
4	Run by the pre-commit hook (.githooks/pre-commit) so every commit pulls in an up-to-date
5	snapshot of the agent interactions/effort behind it â€” an auditable record alongside the work.
6	
7	What it captures: the *active* session (the most-recently-modified top-level transcript in the
8	Claude project dir) plus that session's ``<id>/`` subtree (subagents + workflow transcripts).
9	Other/unrelated sessions are left out. Credential-shaped secrets are redacted on the way in.
10	
11	Honest scope: transcripts still contain local filesystem paths and may contain the operator's
12	email and internal reasoning. If this repo is published, review/exclude ``transcripts/`` first
13	(see transcripts/README.md). The wheel never ships them (only ``src/assay_engine`` is packaged).
14	
15	Config: ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC overrides the source dir. Exits 0 on any error (never blocks a
16	commit).
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import datetime as _dt
22	import hashlib
23	import json
24	import os
25	import re
26	import sys
27	from pathlib import Path
28	
29	DEFAULT_SRC = Path.home() / ".claude" / "projects" / "-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc"
30	DEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "sessions"
31	MANIFEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "MANIFEST.json"
32	
33	# Credential-shaped secrets to redact (defense; does not touch paths/email/reasoning, which are
34	# legitimate provenance â€” see README caveat for the public-release consideration).
35	_SECRET_PATTERNS = [
36	    re.compile(r"sk-ant-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2,}"),  # any sk-ant- token incl. oat01 OAuth + placeholders
37	    re.compile(r"\bgh[posru]_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}\b"),  # GitHub tokens
38	    re.compile(r"\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b"),  # AWS access key id
39	    re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-]{20,}"),
40	    # explicit OAuth/token env values: "...OAUTH_TOKEN":"<value>" / ...KEY=<value>
41	    re.compile(r'((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)"\s*:\s*")[^"]{8,}(")'),
42	    re.compile(r"((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)=)[^\s\"']{8,}"),
43	]
44	
45	
46	def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
47	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
48	        if pat.groups == 2:
49	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
50	        elif pat.groups == 1:
51	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)
52	        else:
53	            text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
54	    return text
55	
56	
57	def _active_session(src: Path) -> Path | None:
58	    """The most-recently-modified top-level transcript = the live session."""
59	    candidates = sorted(src.glob("*.jsonl"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
60	    return candidates[0] if candidates else None
61	
62	
63	def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
64	    try:
65	        dstf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
66	        raw = srcf.read_bytes()
67	        if srcf.suffix == ".jsonl":
68	            data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")
69	        else:
70	            data = raw
71	        if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
72	            pass  # unchanged â€” still report it in the manifest
73	        else:
74	            dstf.write_bytes(data)
75	        return {
76	            "path": str(dstf.relative_to(DEST.parent.parent)),
77	            "bytes": len(data),
78	            "sha256": hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest(),
79	        }
80	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 â€” provenance capture must never break a commit
81	        return None
82	
83	
84	def main() -> int:
85	    src = Path(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC", str(DEFAULT_SRC)))
86	    if not src.is_dir():
87	        return 0
88	    session = _active_session(src)
89	    if session is None:
90	        return 0
91	    sid = session.stem
92	    captured: list[dict] = []
93	
94	    rec = _copy_scrubbed(session, DEST / f"{sid}.jsonl")
95	    if rec:
96	        captured.append(rec)
97	
98	    # the session's subtree: subagents/ + workflows/ transcripts
99	    subtree = src / sid
100	    if subtree.is_dir():
101	        for f in sorted(subtree.rglob("*")):
102	            if f.is_file():
103	                rel = f.relative_to(subtree)
104	                rec = _copy_scrubbed(f, DEST / sid / rel)
105	                if rec:
106	                    captured.append(rec)
107	
108	    try:
109	        MANIFEST.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
110	        MANIFEST.write_text(
111	            json.dumps(
112	                {
113	                    "captured_at": _dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
114	                    "active_session": sid,
115	                    "n_files": len(captured),
116	                    "total_bytes": sum(c["bytes"] for c in captured),
117	                    "files": captured,
118	                },
119	                indent=2,
120	            )
121	            + "\n",
122	            encoding="utf-8",
123	        )
124	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
125	        return 0
126	    print(f"captured {len(captured)} transcript file(s) for session {sid}")
127	    return 0
128	
129	
130	if __name__ == "__main__":
131	    sys.exit(main())
132

1	"""Study runner â€” the composed, governed, end-to-end pipeline (ADR-0010).
2	
3	The phase machine (:mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.phases`) *names* the ordered stages a
4	study moves through; the firewall runners enforce the methodology; the seams provide tracing,
5	experiment tracking, data versioning, and (in the adapter's callables) reasoning. This module
6	composes them into a single runnable flow, engine-owned so the ordering and the governance
7	handoffs are guaranteed once, not re-implemented per study:
8	
9	    INGEST â†’ BASELINE(blind, Firewall A) â†’ [DISCOVERY â†’ PREREGISTER â†’ gate â†’ CONFIRM (Firewall B)]
10	           â†’ [ADJUDICATE â†’ SCORE (Firewall A, shared baseline)] â†’ REPORT
11	
12	At every step it (a) records an append-only provenance entry *before the next step runs*
13	(GOVERNANCE Â§3), (b) opens a trace span (observability seam), optionally (c) versions the source
14	and logs to an experiment tracker, and (d) enforces the methodological invariants structurally:
15	the baseline is built blind, the split ids are validated against the corpus, hypotheses are
16	verifiably pre-registered before confirmation, a human gate reviews before *every* confirmatory
17	step, claim-derived hypotheses are locked before the baseline exists, and the visited phase
18	sequence must equal the mode's required sequence.
19	
20	A study supplies the *domain* via a :class:`StudyPlan` (parser, baseline builder, the
21	discover/confirm/hypothesis_for callables, the claims source, optional feature builders); the
22	engine owns the *order, the firewalls, the gate, the provenance, the tracing, and the
23	versioning*. Reasoning enters only inside the study's own callables (which may use
24	:mod:`assay_engine.reasoning`); any such LLM output is *judgment*, so it should be wrapped as an
25	:class:`~assay_engine.methodology.fence.Interpretation` and never fed back into a measurement
26	(the fence). The runner imports no adapter (ARCHITECTURE Â§3).
27	"""

119	            {
120	                "claim_id": c.claim_id,

247	def run_study(
248	    plan: StudyPlan,
249	    *,
250	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
251	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
252	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
253	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
254	    clock: Clock | None = None,
255	    secret: bytes | None = None,
256	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
257	) -> StudyResult:
258	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
259	
260	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice â€”
261	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
262	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
263	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
264	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
265	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
266	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
267	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
268	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
269	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
270	
271	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
272	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
273	    """
274	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
275	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
276	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
277	    defn = plan.definition
278	    modes = defn.modes
279	    required = required_phases(modes)
280	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
281	    visited: list[Phase] = []
282	    run_id: str | None = None
283	
284	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
285	        visited.append(phase)
286	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
287	
288	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
289	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
290	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
291	            return
292	        try:
293	            fn(tracker, run_id)
294	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 â€” tracker is an external, optional backend
295	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
296	
297	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
298	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
299	        decision = gate_handler(review)
300	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
301	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
302	        trail.record(
303	            "gate",
304	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
305	            gate=review.gate,
306	            approved=approved,
307	            reason=reason,
308	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
309	        )
310	        if not approved:
311	            raise GateError(
312	                f"gate {review.gate!r} blocked {review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}: {reason}"
313	            )
314	
315	    trail.record(
316	        "run_start",
317	        f"study {defn.name!r} starting",
318	        study=defn.name,
319	        modes=sorted(m.value for m in modes),
320	        research_questions=list(defn.research_questions),
321	    )
322	    if tracker is not None:
323	        try:
324	            run_id = tracker.start_run(
325	                defn.name,
326	                {
327	                    "modes": ",".join(sorted(m.value for m in modes)),
328	                    "n_research_questions": len(defn.research_questions),
329	                },
330	            )
331	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
332	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
333	
334	    ok = False
335	    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
336	    try:
337	        # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
338	        # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122).
339	        # Done INSIDE the try so a tracing-setup failure is still covered by the finally's
340	        # tracker.end_run cleanup â€” an already-started tracker run must never leak in RUNNING
341	        # state (the #110 class of bug) if bootstrap/context entry raises (#156). Best-effort.
342	        bootstrap_tracing()
343	        corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex
344	        trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id))
345	        # ---- INGEST ----
346	        with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):
347	            enter(Phase.INGEST)
348	            try:
349	                corpus = defn.parser.parse(plan.source)
350	                source_fp = defn.parser.source_fingerprint(plan.source)
351	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 â€” normalize adapter/IO ingestion failures
352	                raise IngestionError(f"parser failed to ingest {plan.source!r}: {exc}") from exc
353	            # The parser Protocol return type is unenforced at runtime; a misimplemented adapter
354	            # that returns None/list/dict would otherwise slip past the normalizer above and die
355	            # at `corpus.units` with an opaque AttributeError, violating the documented
356	            # "Raises IngestionError on a bad source" contract (#134).
357	            if not isinstance(corpus, Corpus):
358	                raise IngestionError(f"parser returned {type(corpus).__name__}, expected Corpus")
359	            if not isinstance(source_fp, str):
360	                raise IngestionError(
361	                    f"parser.source_fingerprint returned {type(source_fp).__name__}, expected str"
362	                )
363	            if not corpus.units:
364	                raise IngestionError("ingestion produced an empty corpus")
365	            cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
366	            source_version: str | None = None
367	            if versioner is not None:
368	                source_version = versioner.put(str(plan.source))
369	            trail.record(
370	                "ingest",
371	                "parsed source into canonical corpus",
372	                source_fingerprint=source_fp,
373	                corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
374	                n_units=len(corpus.units),
375	                n_relations=len(corpus.relations),
376	                source_version=source_version,
377	            )
378	            # split ids must reference real corpus units (no silent partition drift, audit H4)
379	            if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
380	                assert plan.split is not None
381	                unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
382	                unknown = (set(plan.split.discovery_ids) | set(plan.split.confirm_ids)) - unit_ids
383	                if unknown:
384	                    raise FirewallViolation(
385	                        f"split references {len(unknown)} id(s) absent from the corpus "
386	                        f"(e.g. {sorted(unknown)[:3]}) â€” partition would silently drop units"
387	                    )
388	            # optional dataset features (recorded + returned; the baseline builder is blind-built
389	            # from the corpus, so features are provenance/output, not a baseline input here)
390	            feature_matrices: list[FeatureMatrix] = []
391	            corpus_unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
392	            for fb in defn.feature_builders:
393	                fm = fb.build(corpus)
394	                # features must describe THIS corpus's units â€” no fictional or duplicate ids
395	                # (the feature-side analogue of the split-drift guard above).
396	                if len(set(fm.unit_ids)) != len(fm.unit_ids):
397	                    raise FirewallViolation(
398	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned duplicate unit_ids"
399	                    )
400	                ghost = set(fm.unit_ids) - corpus_unit_ids
401	                if ghost:
402	                    raise FirewallViolation(
403	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned {len(ghost)} unit_id(s) "
404	                        f"absent from the corpus (e.g. {sorted(ghost)[:3]})"
405	                    )
406	                feature_matrices.append(fm)
407	                trail.record(
408	                    "features",
409	                    f"built feature matrix ({len(fm.feature_names)} features)",
410	                    provides=list(fb.provides),
411	                    n_units=len(fm.unit_ids),
412	                    feature_names=list(fm.feature_names),
413	                )
414	
415	        # ---- BASELINE (blind â€” Firewall A). Capture the ordering instant BEFORE the build. ----
416	        baseline_instant = _now()
417	        with tracer.span("phase:BASELINE"):
418	            enter(Phase.BASELINE)
419	            guard: ClaimBlindGuard[Any] = ClaimBlindGuard()
420	            with guard.sealed():
421	                baseline = plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
422	            if baseline.corpus_fingerprint != cfp:
423	                raise FirewallViolation(
424	                    "baseline corpus_fingerprint does not match the ingested corpus â€” the baseline "
425	                    "was not built from this corpus"
426	                )
427	            trail.record(
428	                "baseline",
429	                "built blind baseline (Firewall A)",
430	                corpus_fingerprint=baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
431	                contents=sorted(baseline.contents),
432	                determinism=dict(baseline.determinism),
433	            )
434	
435	        discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...] = ()
436	        scorecard: SourceScorecard | None = None
437	
438	        # ---- DISCOVERY â†’ PREREGISTER â†’ gate â†’ CONFIRM (Firewall B) ----
439	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
440	            assert plan.split is not None and plan.discover is not None
441	            assert plan.confirm_held_out is not None
442	            discovery_corpus = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.discovery_ids)
443	            held_out = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.confirm_ids)
444	
445	            with tracer.span("phase:DISCOVERY"):
446	                enter(Phase.DISCOVERY)
447	                if not discovery_corpus.units:
448	                    raise FirewallViolation("discovery partition selects no corpus units")
449	                hypotheses = list(plan.discover(discovery_corpus))  # only the discovery partition
450	                # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated hypothesis_id would be counted once
451	                # per occurrence in the verdict accounting (#138)
452	                _require_unique_ids([h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses], "hypothesis_id")
453	                trail.record(
454	                    "discovery",
455	                    f"data surfaced {len(hypotheses)} candidate hypotheses",
456	                    n=len(hypotheses),
457	                    ids=[h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
458	                )
459	
460	            with tracer.span("phase:PREREGISTER"):
461	                enter(Phase.PREREGISTER)
462	                if not held_out.units:
463	                    raise FirewallViolation("confirmation partition selects no corpus units")
464	                preregister_instant = _now()
465	                for h in hypotheses:
466	                    if h.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY:
467	                        raise FirewallViolation(
468	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} is {h.origin.value!r}; the discovery "
469	                            "phase expects DISCOVERY-origin (data-surfaced) hypotheses"
470	                        )
471	                    vt = require_preregistered(
472	                        h, authority=plan.authority, not_after=preregister_instant
473	                    )
474	                    trail.record(
475	                        "preregister",
476	                        f"locked hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
477	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
478	                        digest=vt.digest,
479	                        locked_at=vt.instant.isoformat(),
480	                    )
481	                run_gate(
482	                    GateReview(
483	                        gate="review-locked-hypotheses",
484	                        frm=Phase.PREREGISTER,
485	                        to=Phase.CONFIRM,
486	                        summary="review locked hypotheses before confirmatory testing",
487	                        payload={
488	                            "hypothesis_ids": [h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
489	                            "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
490	                        },
491	                    )
492	                )
493	
494	            with tracer.span("phase:CONFIRM"):
495	                enter(Phase.CONFIRM)
496	                confirmed: list[Verdict] = []
497	                for h in hypotheses:
498	                    verdict = plan.confirm_held_out(h, held_out)  # only the held-out partition
499	                    if verdict.hypothesis_id != h.hypothesis_id:
500	                        raise FirewallViolation(
501	                            f"verdict reports hypothesis_id {verdict.hypothesis_id!r} for "
502	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} â€” hypothesisâ†”verdict misattribution"
503	                        )
504	                    confirmed.append(verdict)
505	                    trail.record(
506	                        "verdict",
507	                        f"confirmed {h.hypothesis_id!r}: {verdict.label.value}",
508	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
509	                        label=verdict.label.value,
510	                        rule=verdict.decision_rule,
511	                    )
512	                discovery_verdicts = tuple(confirmed)
513	                for lbl in ("supported", "contradicted", "indeterminate"):
514	                    n = sum(1 for v in discovery_verdicts if v.label.value == lbl)
515	                    track(_count_logger(f"discovery_{lbl}", float(n)))
516	
517	        # ---- ADJUDICATE â†’ SCORE (Firewall A; the baseline built above is reused, blind) ----
518	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
519	            assert defn.claims_source is not None
520	            assert plan.hypothesis_for is not None and plan.confirm_claim is not None
521	            claim_records = list(defn.claims_source.claims())
522	            if not claim_records:
523	                raise ValueError(
524	                    "adjudication mode but the claims source yielded no claims â€” nothing to adjudicate"
525	                )
526	            # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated claim_id would inflate the scorecard (#138)
527	            _require_unique_ids([c.claim_id for c in claim_records], "claim_id")
528	            # The engine fingerprints the EXACT claims it will score (authoritative), and records
529	            # the source's self-report separately for cross-check rather than trusting it (#137).
530	            engine_claim_fp = _engine_claim_fingerprint(claim_records)
531	            source_claim_fp = defn.claims_source.claim_fingerprint()
532	            prev_phase = Phase.CONFIRM if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes else Phase.BASELINE
533	            run_gate(
534	                GateReview(
535	                    gate="review-baseline-and-claims",
536	                    frm=prev_phase,
537	                    to=Phase.ADJUDICATE,
538	                    summary="review the blind baseline and the external claim set before adjudication",
539	                    payload={
540	                        "n_claims": len(claim_records),
541	                        "claim_ids": [c.claim_id for c in claim_records],
542	                        "claim_fingerprint": engine_claim_fp,  # engine-computed over scored claims
543	                        "source_reported_claim_fingerprint": source_claim_fp,  # unverified self-report
544	                        "claim_fingerprint_matches_source": engine_claim_fp == source_claim_fp,
545	                        "baseline_fingerprint": baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
546	                        "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
547	                    },
548	                )
549	            )
550	            with tracer.span("phase:ADJUDICATE"):
551	                enter(Phase.ADJUDICATE)
552	
553	                def _record_adjudication_step(h: Hypothesis, v: Verdict) -> None:
554	                    trail.record(
555	                        "preregister",
556	                        f"locked claim hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
557	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
558	                        source_claim_id=h.source_claim_id,
559	                        locked_at=h.locked_at,
560	                    )
561	                    trail.record(
562	                        "verdict",
563	                        f"adjudicated {v.hypothesis_id!r}: {v.label.value}",
564	                        hypothesis_id=v.hypothesis_id,
565	                        label=v.label.value,
566	                        rule=v.decision_rule,
567	                    )
568	
569	                # not_after = baseline_instant: a claim-derived hypothesis must have been locked
570	                # before the (shared) baseline existed. The SAME materialized claim_records that
571	                # were gated/recorded are scored (#95).
572	                scorecard = adjudicate_with_baseline(
573	                    baseline,
574	                    claim_records,
575	                    hypothesis_for=plan.hypothesis_for,
576	                    confirm=plan.confirm_claim,
577	                    authority=plan.authority,
578	                    not_after=baseline_instant,
579	                    source_name=defn.name,
580	                    on_step=_record_adjudication_step,
581	                )
582	            with tracer.span("phase:SCORE"):
583	                enter(Phase.SCORE)
584	                trail.record(
585	                    "score",
586	                    f"source scorecard for {defn.name!r}",
587	                    n_supported=scorecard.n_supported,
588	                    n_contradicted=scorecard.n_contradicted,
589	                    n_indeterminate=scorecard.n_indeterminate,
590	                    alignment_rate=scorecard.alignment_rate,
591	                )
592	                sc = scorecard
593	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_supported", float(sc.n_supported)))
594	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_contradicted", float(sc.n_contradicted)))
595	                track(
596	                    lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_indeterminate", float(sc.n_indeterminate))
597	                )
598	                ar = sc.alignment_rate
599	                if ar is not None:
600	                    track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "alignment_rate", float(ar)))
601	
602	        # ---- REPORT ----
603	        with tracer.span("phase:REPORT"):
604	            enter(Phase.REPORT)
605	            trail.record(
606	                "report",
607	                "assembled reproducibility package",
608	                n_discovery_verdicts=len(discovery_verdicts),
609	                has_scorecard=scorecard is not None,
610	            )
611	
612	        if tuple(visited) != required:
613	            raise FirewallViolation(
614	                f"visited phase sequence {[p.name for p in visited]} != required "
615	                f"{[p.name for p in required]}"
616	            )
617	        trail.verify()  # the provenance chain must be intact before we hand back the result
618	        ok = True
619	    except Exception as exc:
620	        # Record a terminal failure entry BEFORE re-raising so the abort reason is recoverable
621	        # from the (caller-owned) trail, not lost (#109).
622	        last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start"
623	        try:
624	            trail.record(
625	                "run_failed",
626	                f"{type(exc).__name__} during {last_phase}",
627	                error_type=type(exc).__name__,
628	                message=str(exc)[:2000],
629	                last_phase=last_phase,
630	            )
631	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 â€” never mask the original failure
632	            pass
633	        raise
634	    finally:
635	        trace_ctx.close()
636	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
637	            try:
638	                # a failed run must be distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110)
639	                tracker.end_run(run_id, status="FINISHED" if ok else "FAILED")
640	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 â€” best-effort cleanup
641	                pass
642	
643	    return StudyResult(
644	        study=defn.name,
645	        corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
646	        baseline=baseline,
647	        discovery_verdicts=discovery_verdicts,
648	        scorecard=scorecard,
649	        phases=tuple(visited),
650	        provenance=trail.entries,
651	        source_version=source_version,
652	        feature_matrices=tuple(feature_matrices),
653	        experiment_run_id=run_id,
654	    )

1	"""Phase machine â€” the ordered stages a study moves through.
2	
3	The ordering encodes the method: ingest and build the *blind* baseline before any external
4	claim is touched (Firewall A), and discover before confirm (Firewall B). Gates guard the
5	transitions between these phases.
6	
7	Mode coupling (audit pass 1, issue #9): the legal phase sequence depends on the study's
8	modes, which are independent. A discovery-only study runs INGESTâ†’BASELINEâ†’DISCOVERYâ†’
9	PREREGISTERâ†’CONFIRMâ†’REPORT and must *not* enter the external-claims phases (ADJUDICATE,
10	SCORE); an adjudicate-only study runs INGESTâ†’BASELINEâ†’ADJUDICATEâ†’SCOREâ†’REPORT and never enters
11	the discovery spine; a combined study runs the full sequence. Use :func:`required_phases` /
12	:func:`legal_transition` for mode-aware legality; :meth:`Phase.can_advance_to` is the
13	mode-agnostic full-pipeline adjacency check.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	from enum import Enum
19	
20	from assay_engine.contracts.study import StudyMode
21	
22	
23	class Phase(Enum):
24	    """Ordered analysis phases. Value is the sort key used to forbid backward transitions."""
25	
26	    INGEST = 1  # raw source â†’ canonical corpus
27	    BASELINE = 2  # build the independent baseline, blind to any claims (Firewall A)
28	    DISCOVERY = 3  # data surfaces candidate hypotheses
29	    PREREGISTER = 4  # lock + timestamp hypotheses before confirmation
30	    CONFIRM = 5  # confirmatory tests on held-out / null distributions (Firewall B)
31	    ADJUDICATE = 6  # convert external claims to verdicts against the blind baseline
32	    SCORE = 7  # score the external source against the validated baseline
33	    REPORT = 8  # assemble the reproducibility package
34	
35	    def can_advance_to(self, other: "Phase") -> bool:
36	        """Adjacent in the *full* pipeline (mode-agnostic). For mode-aware legality that lets
37	        a discovery-only study skip ADJUDICATE/SCORE, use :func:`legal_transition`."""
38	        return other.value == self.value + 1
39	
40	
41	def required_phases(modes: frozenset[StudyMode]) -> tuple[Phase, ...]:
42	    """The ordered phases a study with ``modes`` actually visits.
43	
44	    The two modes are independent (a study may declare either or both). INGEST and BASELINE
45	    always run; the discovery spine (DISCOVERYâ†’PREREGISTERâ†’CONFIRM) runs only in DISCOVERY mode;
46	    the external-claims phases (ADJUDICATEâ†’SCORE) run only in adjudication mode; REPORT always
47	    closes. So an adjudicate-only study goes INGESTâ†’BASELINEâ†’ADJUDICATEâ†’SCOREâ†’REPORT and never
48	    enters the discovery spine (it derives hypotheses from claims, not from the data).
49	    """
50	    seq = [Phase.INGEST, Phase.BASELINE]
51	    if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
52	        seq += [Phase.DISCOVERY, Phase.PREREGISTER, Phase.CONFIRM]
53	    if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
54	        seq += [Phase.ADJUDICATE, Phase.SCORE]
55	    seq.append(Phase.REPORT)
56	    return tuple(seq)
57	
58	
59	def legal_transition(frm: Phase, to: Phase, modes: frozenset[StudyMode]) -> bool:
60	    """True iff ``to`` immediately follows ``frm`` in the phase sequence for ``modes``.
61	
62	    A discovery-only study therefore legally goes CONFIRMâ†’REPORT and can never enter
63	    ADJUDICATE/SCORE; an adjudication study goes CONFIRMâ†’ADJUDICATEâ†’SCOREâ†’REPORT.
64	    """
65	    seq = required_phases(modes)
66	    try:
67	        i = seq.index(frm)
68	    except ValueError:
69	        return False
70	    return i + 1 < len(seq) and seq[i + 1] == to

1	"""Tiered reasoning seam â€” the single abstraction over LLM execution by stakes tier.
2	
3	No engine component talks to an LLM directly; everything routes through :class:`ReasoningSeam`.
4	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's hardened ``reasoning_client`` (ARCHITECTURE.md
5	Â§1, ADR-0003). Two execution tiers:
6	
7	- ``StakesTier.BULK`` â€” a local model runtime (loopback-only, e.g. Ollama) for high-volume,
8	  low-stakes work.
9	- ``StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES`` â€” a frontier model via a **fixed-cost subscription** path
10	  (the ``claude`` CLI / Agent SDK using an OAuth token). No metered API key is ever used:
11	  every metered Anthropic credential is scrubbed from the subprocess environment first.
12	
13	Hardened invariants preserved from the prior platform:
14	
15	- forward-proof credential scrubbing (any ``ANTHROPIC_*KEY``/``*TOKEN`` stripped; the
16	  subscription ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` preserved);
17	- hard per-call timeouts via a bounded worker pool with a saturation guard;
18	- independent retry budgets â€” exponential backoff for transient errors, long backpressure
19	  for rate limits â€” and permanent errors never retried;
20	- robust JSON extraction via a balanced-brace walker (not a greedy regex);
21	- loopback enforcement on the local backend (ADR-0003).
22	
23	Heavy backends (``langchain_ollama``, ``claude_agent_sdk``) and tracing (``opentelemetry``)
24	are imported lazily, so this module imports â€” and is unit-testable by mocking ``_attempt`` â€”
25	with none of them installed. Install the optional ``reasoning`` extra to run real calls.
26	"""

116	# Credential scrubbing (ADR-0003 / no metered API) â€” pure, forward-proof

118	def is_metered_anthropic_credential(key: str) -> bool:
119	    """True for any env var the Anthropic SDK would read as a METERED credential.
120	
121	    Matches the *shape* â€” any ``ANTHROPIC_*`` var naming a ``KEY`` or ``TOKEN`` â€” rather than
122	    a static list, so a newly-added metered var cannot silently slip through. The subscription
123	    ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` does not start with ``ANTHROPIC_`` and is deliberately kept.
124	    """
125	    return key.startswith("ANTHROPIC_") and ("KEY" in key or "TOKEN" in key)

128	# Env vars that â€” beyond metered credentials â€” would redirect the subprocess OFF-BOX or onto a
129	# metered provider, defeating ADR-0003 / "no metered API". The claude CLI honours all of these.
130	_UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS = frozenset(
131	    {
132	        "ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL",
133	        "ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL",
134	        "ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL",
135	        "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK",
136	        "CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX",
137	        "CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER",
138	        "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS",
139	    }
140	)

143	def is_unsafe_subprocess_var(key: str) -> bool:
144	    """True for any env var that must not reach the high-stakes subprocess (#101).
145	
146	    Covers metered credentials, off-box/metered-provider redirects, proxies (which redirect
147	    egress), and cloud-provider credentials (AWS_*/GOOGLE_*). The subscription OAuth token is
148	    deliberately NOT matched.
149	    """
150	    ku = key.upper()
151	    return (
152	        is_metered_anthropic_credential(key)
153	        or ku in _UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS
154	        or ku.endswith("_PROXY")  # HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY/ALL_PROXY/NO_PROXY (any case)
155	        or ku.startswith("AWS_")
156	        or ku.startswith("GOOGLE_")
157	    )

160	def scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
161	    """The process env hardened for the high-stakes subprocess (ADR-0003 / no metered API).
162	
163	    Every unsafe var (:func:`is_unsafe_subprocess_var`) is **overwritten to empty**, not merely
164	    omitted. The claude-agent-sdk builds the child env as ``{**os.environ, **options.env}`` â€” it
165	    *merges* over the inherited environment â€” so a dropped key would silently fall through to its
166	    inherited value (the metered key / off-box redirect would survive). Setting it to ``""`` in
167	    the dict we hand over guarantees the merge overwrites the inherited value (#101). The
168	    subscription OAuth token is preserved so the fixed-cost path still authenticates.
169	    """
170	    env = dict(os.environ)
171	    for k in list(env):
172	        if is_unsafe_subprocess_var(k):
173	            env[k] = ""
174	    return env

177	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
178	# Bounded timeout pool with saturation guard
179	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
180	_POOL_WORKERS = 8
181	_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout")
182	_inflight = 0
183	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
184	
185	
186	def _release(_future: Any) -> None:
187	    global _inflight
188	    with _inflight_lock:
189	        _inflight -= 1
190	
191	
192	def _submit_bounded(fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> Any:
193	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if every worker slot is already in flight
194	    (hung backend calls would otherwise leak all slots)."""
195	    global _inflight
196	    with _inflight_lock:
197	        if _inflight >= _POOL_WORKERS:
198	            # Saturation is a TRANSIENT condition â€” surface a retryable backpressure signal so
199	            # _run_with_retries waits and re-tries rather than failing the caller permanently
200	            # (#104). Concurrent callers thus queue briefly instead of erroring out.
201	            raise RateLimitError(
202	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_POOL_WORKERS} in-flight) â€” "
203	                "backpressure; retry shortly"
204	            )
205	        _inflight += 1
206	    try:
207	        future = _pool.submit(fn)
208	    except RuntimeError as exc:  # pool shut down at interpreter exit â€” normalize, don't leak (#116)
209	        with _inflight_lock:
210	            _inflight -= 1
211	        raise PermanentReasoningError(f"reasoning pool is shut down: {exc}") from exc
212	    except BaseException:
213	        with _inflight_lock:
214	            _inflight -= 1
215	        raise
216	    future.add_done_callback(_release)
217	    return future
218	
219	
220	def _with_timeout(fn: Callable[[], Any], timeout: float, what: str) -> Any:
221	    """Run a sync callable with a hard wall-clock timeout via the bounded pool."""
222	    future = _submit_bounded(fn)
223	    try:
224	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
225	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
226	        # No future.cancel(): a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked
227	        # in a backend call. The worker keeps its slot until the call returns; the bounded
228	        # pool's saturation guard is the real protection against leaked/hung slots.
229	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc

232	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
233	# JSON extraction â€” balanced-brace walker (robust to fences / trailing prose)
234	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
235	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
236	    """Return the first balanced ``{...}`` or ``[...]`` span, respecting string literals.
237	
238	    A greedy regex grabs from the first brace to the LAST brace, mangling replies with
239	    multiple JSON values or braces in trailing prose; this walks the structure honestly.
240	    """
241	    start = None
242	    opener = closer = ""
243	    depth = 0
244	    in_string = False
245	    escape = False
246	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
247	        if start is None:
248	            if ch in "{[":
249	                start, opener = i, ch
250	                closer = "}" if ch == "{" else "]"
251	                depth = 1
252	            continue
253	        if in_string:
254	            if escape:
255	                escape = False
256	            elif ch == "\\":
257	                escape = True
258	            elif ch == '"':
259	                in_string = False
260	            continue
261	        if ch == '"':
262	            in_string = True
263	        elif ch == opener:
264	            depth += 1
265	        elif ch == closer:
266	            depth -= 1
267	            if depth == 0:
268	                return text[start : i + 1]
269	    return None
270	
271	
272	def extract_json(text: str) -> Any:
273	    """Parse JSON from a model reply (handles code fences and surrounding prose)."""
274	    span = _first_balanced_json(text)
275	    if span is None:
276	        raise ReasoningError("no JSON object/array found in reply")
277	    try:
278	        return json.loads(span)
279	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
280	        raise ReasoningError(f"reply contained malformed JSON: {exc}") from exc

286	def _bulk_complete(
287	    prompt: str,
288	    system: str | None,
289	    temperature: float,
290	    model: str,
291	    json_mode: bool = False,
292	    *,
293	    seed: int | None = None,
294	    json_schema: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
295	) -> str:
296	    """Tier: BULK â€” local model runtime (loopback-enforced).
297	
298	    ``num_predict`` is always set (bounded generation). For JSON, a caller-supplied
299	    ``json_schema`` uses Ollama's schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path); otherwise
300	    ``json_mode`` falls back to loose ``format="json"``. ``seed`` lets a retry force a different
301	    deterministic decode without sharpening temperature.
302	    """
303	    require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
304	    try:
305	        from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
306	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
307	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
308	            "bulk tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (langchain-ollama) â€” not installed"
309	        ) from exc
310	
311	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = dict(
312	        model=model,
313	        base_url=BULK_BASE_URL,
314	        temperature=temperature,
315	        num_predict=BULK_NUM_PREDICT,  # bound generation length (H1)
316	        client_kwargs={"timeout": BULK_TIMEOUT},
317	    )
318	    if seed is not None:
319	        kwargs["seed"] = seed
320	    if json_schema is not None:
321	        kwargs["format"] = json_schema  # Ollama schema-constrained decoding (reliable JSON)
322	    elif json_mode:
323	        kwargs["format"] = "json"  # loose native JSON decoding (syntactic only)
324	    client = ChatOllama(**kwargs)
325	    messages: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
326	    if system:
327	        messages.append(("system", system))
328	    messages.append(("human", prompt))
329	    try:
330	        reply = client.invoke(messages)
331	    except Exception as exc:
332	        # Prefer the typed HTTP status (ollama.ResponseError / httpx carry one): 404 = the model
333	        # isn't pulled â†’ permanent. Fall back to substring matching only if no status is exposed
334	        # (robust to upstream message-wording changes â€” audit M4).
335	        status = getattr(exc, "status_code", None) or getattr(
336	            getattr(exc, "response", None), "status_code", None
337	        )
338	        msg = str(exc).lower()
339	        permanent = status == 404 or any(
340	            s in msg for s in ("not found", "no such model", "try pulling")
341	        )
342	        if permanent:
343	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"bulk model {model!r} not available: {exc}") from exc
344	        raise ReasoningError(f"bulk tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
345	    content = getattr(reply, "content", reply)
346	    text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
347	    text = text.strip()
348	    if not text:  # an empty reply is not a valid result â€” retry, don't return "" (issue #5/R5)
349	        raise ReasoningError("bulk tier returned an empty reply")
350	    return text
351

353	def _high_stakes_auth_present() -> bool:
354	    """Subscription auth available: an exported OAuth token OR a stored CLI credentials file
355	    (the normal state after ``claude login`` without exporting a token)."""
356	    if os.environ.get(OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV):
357	        return True
358	    return (Path.home() / ".claude" / ".credentials.json").is_file()
